# mdpress - LLM Quick Reference

> Turn Markdown and AI output into branded PDFs

## Syntax Overview

Variables (Pro feature, requires --data flag):
  {{ variable }}           Basic interpolation
  {{ nested.path }}        Nested object access
  {{#items}}...{{/items}}  Loops/sections
  {{^cond}}...{{/cond}}    Inverted conditionals

Directives:
  {{@pagebreak}}                    Force page break
  {{@columns}}...{{/columns}}       Multi-column layout
  {{@toc}}                          Table of contents
  {{@note}}...{{/note}}             Note callout box
  {{@warning}}...{{/warning}}       Warning callout box
  {{@highlight}}...{{/highlight}}   Highlighted section

## Authoring Rules

1. Prefer standard Markdown for all formatting
2. Place directives on their own line (never inline)
3. Use variables only with --data flag and Pro license
4. Unknown directives cause errors - use only documented ones

## Examples

Good:
  # Invoice for {{ client.name }}

  {{@note}}
  Payment due within 30 days.
  {{/note}}

  {{@pagebreak}}

  ## Line Items
  {{#items}}
  - {{ description }}: ${{ amount }}
  {{/items}}

Bad:
  Total: {{@highlight}}$500{{/highlight}}  <- inline directive
  {{@custom}}                               <- unknown directive
  <style>body{color:red}</style>            <- raw CSS unsupported

## Full Reference

See /llms-full.txt for complete directive reference, worked examples, and agent instructions.
